content:
Anyone can easily make an AI news website today. They can easily use AI to paraphrase or rewrite the work of a human journalist. But this should run into copyright issues if you are making this your entire news outlet.
This now leads to an important question. If AI is searching people's websites and then using that content by presenting it to the user, should that not be considered a serious crime? You have robbed someone of internet traffic by essentially paraphrasing their work. You have stolen their work and presented it as an AI response.
Does anyone else understand the point I'm making and how egregious AI searches are and why action should be taken against them?

  submitted by   /u/Ordinary_Count_203
[link]   [comments]
